html:
The Sign is a craftable Structure. While it cannot be written on, it shows up as an icon on the Map. As such, it can be used as a marker for areas of interest which do not show up on the Map (such as Blue Mushrooms and/or Mushroom Rings, or Beefalo herds).

The Sign requires 1 set of Boards to craft and a Science Machine to prototype. If it is destroyed, the Boards are returned, allowing the player to build a new Sign elsewhere.

Trivia

  • Signs were added in A Little Rain Must Fall update.
  • Naturally occurring Signs can be found in Adventure Mode, usually near Maxwell's Tooth Traps.
  • One can craft Signs for free by clicking the Boards and then crafting a Sign while having the boards still selected.
  • Despite Wolfgang not being able to read, in Don't Starve Together he is able to do so.

Gallery

  • A Sign marking a Mushroom ring.
  • Maxwell's Tooth Traps and a couple of world-spawned Signs.
  • In Hamlet, the Relic Thing's Set Piece contains a Sign.
  • Old Crafting Menu Sign Icon
  • Sign Burnt
Craftable ItemsStructures

"homesign"
“It says 'You are here'.”
–Wilson
“Less reading! More burning!”
–Willow
“Is no time for reading signs!”
–Wolfgang
“A mark is made here, however transient...”
–Wendy
“YOU ARE HERE”
–WX-78
“A navigational aid.”
–Wickerbottom
“Is there something wood can't do?”
–Woodie
“It says... nothing.”
–Maxwell
“Curious. It's somehow entangled with my map.”
–Wagstaff
“A möst well placed sign.”
–Wigfrid
“We could write on that if only we had a pen!”
–Webber
“Hang ten.”
–Walani
“What's the use in a sign around here?”
–Warly
“Must be somethin' important 'round 'ere.”
–Woodlegs
“IT SAYETH WORDS.”
–Wilba
“Words on friends”
–Wormwood
“It's a sign. Literally and figuratively.”
–Wheeler
“I'll take this as a sign.”
–Winona
“"You are here" it says, it says.”
–Wortox
“What it say?”
–Wurt
“Someone has bad handwriting.”
–Walter
“That must be some kind of sign.”
–Wanda
The Sign is a craftable Structure. While it cannot be written on, it shows up as an icon on the Map. As such, it can be used as a marker for areas of interest which do not show up on the Map (such as Blue Mushrooms and/or Mushroom Rings, or Beefalo herds).
The Sign requires 1 set of Boards to craft and a Science Machine to prototype. If it is destroyed, the Boards are returned, allowing the player to build a new Sign elsewhere.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Signs were added in A Little Rain Must Fall update.
Naturally occurring Signs can be found in Adventure Mode, usually near Maxwell's Tooth Traps.
One can craft Signs for free by clicking the Boards and then crafting a Sign while having the boards still selected.
Despite Wolfgang not being able to read, in Don't Starve Together he is able to do so.
Gallery[edit | edit source]
A Sign marking a Mushroom ring.
Maxwell's Tooth Traps and a couple of world-spawned Signs.
In Hamlet, the Relic Thing's Set Piece contains a Sign.

The '''Sign''' is a craftable [[Structure]]. While it cannot be written on, it shows up as an icon on the [[Map]]. As such, it can be used as a marker for areas of interest which do not show up on the Map (such as [[Blue Mushroom]]s and/or Mushroom Rings, or [[Beefalo]] herds).

The Sign requires 1 set of [[Boards]] to craft and a [[Science Machine]] to prototype. If it is destroyed, the [[Boards]] are returned, allowing the player to build a new Sign elsewhere.

== {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Signs were added in ''[[Version History|A Little Rain Must Fall]]'' update.
* Naturally occurring Signs can be found in [[Adventure Mode]], usually near [[Maxwell's Tooth Trap]]s.
* One can craft Signs for free by clicking the Boards and then crafting a Sign while having the boards still selected.
* Despite [[Wolfgang]] not being able to read, in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' he is able to do so.
